act_name: The Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act
section_title: False statement by supplier.—(1) Where a person who is not registered
content:
75. False statement by supplier.—(1) Where a person who is not registered
makes a supply of a good to the recipient of a supply and issues a false document
purporting to be a tax invoice, which makes such supply appear like a taxable supply,
such supply shall be treated as a taxable supply, and if such person is registered, the
applicable rate would be the rate that would have been applicable to such supply:
Provided that if a higher rate is shown on, or is understood from, the documents,
such higher rate shall be applicable.
Page 43 10/21/2013
(2) The Commissioner shall, after giving such person an opportunity of being
heard, treat such person as a registered person and make a tax
determination treating such supply as a taxable supply.

sub_sections.text:
Where a person who is not registered
makes a supply of a good to the recipient of a supply and issues a false document
purporting to be a tax invoice, which makes such supply appear like a taxable supply,
such supply shall be treated as a taxable supply, and if such person is registered, the
applicable rate would be the rate that would have been applicable to such supply:
Provided that if a higher rate is shown on, or is understood from, the documents,
such higher rate shall be applicable.
Page 43 10/21/2013

The Commissioner shall, after giving such person an opportunity of being
heard, treat such person as a registered person and make a tax
determination treating such supply as a taxable supply.
